channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: The Iran War Is Crippling One of the World’s Wealthiest Nations
item.description: Iranian attacks and the stoppage of seaborne transit have paralyzed Qatar’s vital gas exports, stalling the economic pivots intended to anchor the country’s growth.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Qatar has tried to transform itself into a tourist destination and a hub for international business and finance.

item.title: In Iraqi Desert, Two Israeli Outposts Were Kept Secret for Months
item.description: Israel spent over a year preparing a covert site in Iraq for its operations against Iran, regional officials say. Iraqi officials later confirmed the existence of a second base.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Falih Hassan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A shepherd with his herd in the Najaf desert in southwestern Iraq on Tuesday. Israel operated at least two secret outposts in the desert during the war with Iran.

item.title: To Critics, Trump Remarks Reveal a Billionaire Out of Touch
item.description: The president has never pretended to be an ordinary American, but a recent “truth bomb” has opened him to criticism that he doesn’t grasp the economic strain of his war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green
item.media:description: President Trump made remarks on Tuesday at the White House, ahead of departing for his trip to China.

item.title: Trump Administration Pushes Narrative of Christian Nation at Prayer Rally in D.C.
item.description: The nine-hour prayer event features speakers from President Trump’s cabinet and a program that connects the nation’s founding with Christianity.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Dias and Ruth Graham
item.category: Americans United for Separation of Church and State
item.media:credit: Alex Kent for The New York Times
item.media:description: People rose their hands in worship during Rededicate 250: A National Jubilee of Prayer, Praise & Thanksgiving, in Washington on Sunday.

item.title: Conspiracy Trial Will Test Trump’s Aggressive Tactics Against Protesters
item.description: Prosecutors in Spokane, Wash., are trying three activists who they say conspired to impede federal officers. Legal experts call it a stretch.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin
item.media:description: Protestors surrounded a dumpster that was set on fire in front of the Henry M. Jackson Federal Building during a protest against federal immigration arrest last year in Seattle.

item.title: Inside the Secret History of the DeGrange Family
item.description: One fateful decision 100 years ago created parallel lives. How does a family broken by the bizarre rules of racism heal itself after three generations apart?
item.dc:creator: Susan Saulny
item.media:credit: Camille Farrah Lenain for The New York Times
item.media:description: Susan Saulny, granddaughter of George DeGrange, and Christine DeGrange and Laura Oswald, granddaughters of Edward DeGrange, at the building that formerly held the Sisters of the Holy Family convent.

item.title: How a Jared Polis Came to Release Election Denier Tina Peters from Prison
item.description: Gov. Jared Polis’s decision to commute the sentence of Tina Peters came after months of tense discussions, including one with President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti and Jack Healy
item.media:credit: Natalie Behring for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tina Peters speaking at a Trump rally in Wyoming in 2022.

item.title: Why Neutral Maps Could Empower Black Voters as Much as the Voting Rights Act
item.description: Our simulations debunk the notion that the act created artificial minority districts that wouldn’t otherwise exist.
item.dc:creator: Nate Cohn and Eve Washington

item.title: Trump Tightens Grasp on G.O.P. as Cassidy Loss Shows Cost of Defiance
item.description: The defeat showed the president’s dominance in his party, even as a broader range of views about Mr. Trump could be a major Republican liability in the midterms.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Friday.

item.title: What to Know About Letlow And Fleming in the Louisiana Senate Race
item.description: He was defeated in the Republican primary in Louisiana on Saturday. Representative Julia Letlow and State Treasurer John Fleming are now in a runoff for the party’s nomination.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Annie Flanagan for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Julia Letlow at her election watch party in Baton Rouge, La.

item.title: Inside the Met Opera: Does Peter Gelb Have ‘the Most Difficult Job on Earth’?
item.description: Peter Gelb, the general manager of the Metropolitan Opera, said he is not going anywhere as he struggles with financial crises and opera’s place in a changing arts landscape.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nagourney

item.title: The Quest for Clean Hydrogen Moves Underground
item.description: The dream of clean hydrogen has tantalized energy experts for years, but producing it has been tough. Many start-ups think the answer could lie beneath our feet.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer and Ian Willms

item.title: How the Reputation Firm Terakeet Failed to Fix an Epstein Friend’s Image
item.description: Terakeet, a reputation management firm, used online tricks to downplay the friendship of the Goldman Sachs general counsel, Kathryn Ruemmler, with the sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. It wasn’t enough.
item.dc:creator: Robert Draper
item.category: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform

item.title: The Big Questions About Jeffrey Epstein: What The Times Has Learned
item.description: Here is what we know about the sexual predator whose secrets spurred an international reckoning over money, power and complicity. Check back for updates.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: W.H.O. Declares Ebola Outbreak a Global Health Emergency
item.description: Just a day after the announcement, cases were confirmed in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda. There is no approved vaccine for this species of Ebola.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang, Matthew Mpoke Bigg and Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Africa Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: A man is carried from an ambulance on Saturday as he arrives at a hospital after confirmation of an Ebola outbreak in the Ituri province of the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: Seattle’s Socialist Mayor Taunts the Rich as Rift With Starbucks Widens
item.description: Seattle voters elected Mayor Katie Wilson as tensions rose over wealth inequality, but as Starbucks, one of the city’s most iconic companies, expands in Nashville, she is finding her limits.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin and Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: M. Scott Brauer for The New York Times
item.media:description: Starbucks workers were among many union workers at the annual May Day rally and march celebrating International Workers’ Day in Seattle on May 1.

item.title: Why So Many Guys Are Obsessed With Testosterone
item.description: From the Trump administration to online influencers, the hormone is increasingly seen as the key to achieving a new male ideal.
item.dc:creator: Azeen Ghorayshi

item.title: What It’s Like to Own a Tank or Other Former Military Vehicles
item.description: Where do battle tanks and military trucks go when their service has ended? Enthusiasts and professionals put them to work for search and rescue, marketing and just having fun.
item.dc:creator: Mercedes Lilienthal
item.media:credit: Chase Castor for The New York Times
item.media:description: Westen Champlin in his 1962 Centurion battle tank. He breaks it out for parades and “doing cool stuff” with, he said.

item.title: The Shared Feeling of Being Harvested by the Future
item.description: There is a shared sense of precarity that lies beneath the envy and distrust.
item.dc:creator: Yi-Ling Liu

item.title: Here’s the Trade Deal That Trump and Xi Should Have Reached
item.description: For this to happen, however, both men would need personality transplants.
item.dc:creator: Soumaya Keynes and Chad P. Bown
item.media:credit: Daniel Ribar for The New York Times

item.title: Erdogan Wants Turkey to Have More Babies. Few Parents Are Listening.
item.description: Using cash grants and subsidized loans, President Recep Tayyip Erdogan’s government is trying to increase the birthrate. It’s not working.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard and Safak Timur
item.media:credit: Emin Ozmen for The New York Times
item.media:description: A family visiting Miniaturk, a theme park in Istanbul. Living costs have undermined efforts to increase fertility rates.

item.title: Medicare Coverage for GLP-1 Weight Loss Drugs: Cost, Eligibility and What to Know
item.description: What you should know about the federal government’s pilot program offering GLP-1s solely for weight loss.
item.dc:creator: Mark Miller
item.media:description: The list of GLP-1s that can be prescribed for obesity includes Eli Lilly’s Foundayo, Wegovy in injectable and tablet forms, and Eli Lilly’s Zepbound KwikPen.

item.title: Straus Family Creamery Issues Voluntary Ice Cream Recall
item.description: Straus Family Creamery asked customers to throw out some tubs sold in May across 17 states. No injuries have been reported, the company said.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.media:description: Straus Family Creamery voluntarily recalled some tubs of its organic ice cream, including strawberry pints and quarts, which were on sale in May.

item.title: Two Military Jets Collide in Midair at Idaho Air Show
item.description: Video shared on social media showed the jets striking each other and spinning toward the ground. Four crew members safely ejected before the planes crashed, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz and Edgar Sandoval
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
Gunfighter Skies Mountain Home Air Force Base
item.media:credit: U.S. Air Force photo by Senior Airman Xavier Wilson
item.media:description: The U.S. Air Force demonstration squadron Thunderbirds at the Gunfighter Skies air show at Mountain Home Air Force Base in Idaho on Sunday. Two EA-18G Growler jets that were performing in a demonstration collided.
